key_insight: A fully decoupled SAV-finite element formulation makes the CHNS system linear, efficiently solvable, and unconditionally energy-stable while preserving accuracy.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical and robust time-marching scheme for simulating coupled multiphase fluid flows with interface dynamics in engineering applications.
method: Use a single scalar auxiliary variable to reformulate all nonlinear CHNS terms.
Apply IMEX Euler in time with implicit linear terms and explicit nonlinear terms.
Discretize in space with finite elements, yielding two linear subproblems plus one scalar quadratic equation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports unconditional discrete energy dissipation, stability in several norms, and optimal-order L2 error estimates, all verified by numerical experiments.
watch_item: Although the scheme is linear and decoupled, it still requires solving a scalar quadratic equation and careful finite-element implementation to preserve the stated stability properties.
paper_title: A linear, fully decoupled, and unconditionally energy-stable SAV-FEM for the Cahn--Hilliard--Navier--Stokes model
